metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches. [Analysis](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/analyses/index.html). [Technology, Media & Telecom](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/technologie/index.html). 25/09/2025 • 8 minutes reading. [Julia Krauwer](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/julia-krauwer/index.html). The European technology sector is struggling to scale potential winners due to a lack of growth capital. Yet the continent has a wide range of innovative players. In the Netherlands, for example, almost two-thirds of investments in young software companies go to parties that apply artificial intelligence. In 2024, young European technology companies will collectively raise around $54 billion in venture capital.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: The European technology sector is struggling to scale potential winners due to a lack of growth capital. Yet the continent has a wide range of innovative players. In the Netherlands, for example, almost two-thirds of investments in young software companies go to parties that apply artificial intelligence. In 2024, young European technology companies will collectively raise around $54 billion in venture capital. Almost half of this, more than 23 billion, flowed to Software-as-a-Service (SaaS).

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: Yet the continent has a wide range of innovative players. In the Netherlands, for example, almost two-thirds of investments in young software companies go to parties that apply artificial intelligence. In 2024, young European technology companies will collectively raise around $54 billion in venture capital. Almost half of this, more than 23 billion, flowed to Software-as-a-Service (SaaS). This makes SaaS the largest category, larger than digital marketplaces or companies that develop physical technology.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: In the Netherlands, for example, almost two-thirds of investments in young software companies go to parties that apply artificial intelligence. In 2024, young European technology companies will collectively raise around $54 billion in venture capital. Almost half of this, more than 23 billion, flowed to Software-as-a-Service (SaaS). This makes SaaS the largest category, larger than digital marketplaces or companies that develop physical technology. For investors, the combination of stable subscriptions and scalability is particularly attractive.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: In 2024, young European technology companies will collectively raise around $54 billion in venture capital. Almost half of this, more than 23 billion, flowed to Software-as-a-Service (SaaS). This makes SaaS the largest category, larger than digital marketplaces or companies that develop physical technology. For investors, the combination of stable subscriptions and scalability is particularly attractive. Abundant capital flowed into technology during the coronavirus pandemic, helped by low interest rates and an investor search for scalable business models.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: Almost half of this, more than 23 billion, flowed to Software-as-a-Service (SaaS). This makes SaaS the largest category, larger than digital marketplaces or companies that develop physical technology. For investors, the combination of stable subscriptions and scalability is particularly attractive. Abundant capital flowed into technology during the coronavirus pandemic, helped by low interest rates and an investor search for scalable business models. SaaS benefited from this above average.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: This makes SaaS the largest category, larger than digital marketplaces or companies that develop physical technology. For investors, the combination of stable subscriptions and scalability is particularly attractive. Abundant capital flowed into technology during the coronavirus pandemic, helped by low interest rates and an investor search for scalable business models. SaaS benefited from this above average. However, from 2022 onwards, inflation took hold and central banks raised interest rates, making capital more expensive and correcting valuations.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: For investors, the combination of stable subscriptions and scalability is particularly attractive. Abundant capital flowed into technology during the coronavirus pandemic, helped by low interest rates and an investor search for scalable business models. SaaS benefited from this above average. However, from 2022 onwards, inflation took hold and central banks raised interest rates, making capital more expensive and correcting valuations. That made investors more cautious, especially with mega deals and loss-making growth companies.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: Abundant capital flowed into technology during the coronavirus pandemic, helped by low interest rates and an investor search for scalable business models. SaaS benefited from this above average. However, from 2022 onwards, inflation took hold and central banks raised interest rates, making capital more expensive and correcting valuations. That made investors more cautious, especially with mega deals and loss-making growth companies. Meanwhile, the volumes of venture capital investments appear to be stabilizing.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

SaaS remains the focus of European tech financing, although within this category capital is increasingly going to companies that have AI woven into their core.

text: SaaS remains the focus of European tech financing, although within this category capital is increasingly going to companies that have AI woven into their core. The scalability gap. Within Europe, the United Kingdom attracts the most SaaS investments with more than $77 billion in ten years – more than a third of the European total. metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: The scalability gap. Within Europe, the United Kingdom attracts the most SaaS investments with more than $77 billion in ten years – more than a third of the European total. France and Germany follow with 35 billion dollars (16 percent) and 33 billion (15 percent) respectively. The Netherlands is in stable fourth place with 9.9 billion, accounting for a share of 4.5 percent of all European SaaS investments between 2015 and 2025. Unfortunately, Europe faces a persistent problem: the lack of capital for tech companies to grow into internationally competitive companies.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: Within Europe, the United Kingdom attracts the most SaaS investments with more than $77 billion in ten years – more than a third of the European total. France and Germany follow with 35 billion dollars (16 percent) and 33 billion (15 percent) respectively. The Netherlands is in stable fourth place with 9.9 billion, accounting for a share of 4.5 percent of all European SaaS investments between 2015 and 2025. Unfortunately, Europe faces a persistent problem: the lack of capital for tech companies to grow into internationally competitive companies. American startups are twice as likely to raise a round of more than $15 million than their European counterparts, according to the [State of European Tech report](https://www.stateofeuropeantech.com/chapters/investment-levels) from 2024.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: France and Germany follow with 35 billion dollars (16 percent) and 33 billion (15 percent) respectively. The Netherlands is in stable fourth place with 9.9 billion, accounting for a share of 4.5 percent of all European SaaS investments between 2015 and 2025. Unfortunately, Europe faces a persistent problem: the lack of capital for tech companies to grow into internationally competitive companies. American startups are twice as likely to raise a round of more than $15 million than their European counterparts, according to the [State of European Tech report](https://www.stateofeuropeantech.com/chapters/investment-levels) from 2024. The share of young companies that manage to progress to the scale-up phase is therefore much smaller in Europe.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: The Netherlands is in stable fourth place with 9.9 billion, accounting for a share of 4.5 percent of all European SaaS investments between 2015 and 2025. Unfortunately, Europe faces a persistent problem: the lack of capital for tech companies to grow into internationally competitive companies. American startups are twice as likely to raise a round of more than $15 million than their European counterparts, according to the [State of European Tech report](https://www.stateofeuropeantech.com/chapters/investment-levels) from 2024. The share of young companies that manage to progress to the scale-up phase is therefore much smaller in Europe. According to the researchers, the cumulative deficit now stands at $375 billion – the total amount that young tech companies would have raised over the past decade if access to larger investments were as easy as in the United States (US).

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: Unfortunately, Europe faces a persistent problem: the lack of capital for tech companies to grow into internationally competitive companies. American startups are twice as likely to raise a round of more than $15 million than their European counterparts, according to the [State of European Tech report](https://www.stateofeuropeantech.com/chapters/investment-levels) from 2024. The share of young companies that manage to progress to the scale-up phase is therefore much smaller in Europe. According to the researchers, the cumulative deficit now stands at $375 billion – the total amount that young tech companies would have raised over the past decade if access to larger investments were as easy as in the United States (US). In the US, a large part of the money even goes to rounds above 100 million dollars, with a dominant role for mega deals above 250 million.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: American startups are twice as likely to raise a round of more than $15 million than their European counterparts, according to the [State of European Tech report](https://www.stateofeuropeantech.com/chapters/investment-levels) from 2024. The share of young companies that manage to progress to the scale-up phase is therefore much smaller in Europe. According to the researchers, the cumulative deficit now stands at $375 billion – the total amount that young tech companies would have raised over the past decade if access to larger investments were as easy as in the United States (US). In the US, a large part of the money even goes to rounds above 100 million dollars, with a dominant role for mega deals above 250 million. Although there are not so many fewer SaaS deals being closed in Europe – last year there were 4,616, versus 5,040 in the US – the focus remains on smaller rounds.

In the US, a large part of the money even goes to rounds above 100 million dollars, with a dominant role for mega deals above 250 million. Although there are not so many fewer SaaS deals being closed in Europe – last year there were 4,616, versus 5,040 in the US – the focus remains on smaller rounds. Broadly applicable SaaS niches.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: According to the researchers, the cumulative deficit now stands at $375 billion – the total amount that young tech companies would have raised over the past decade if access to larger investments were as easy as in the United States (US). In the US, a large part of the money even goes to rounds above 100 million dollars, with a dominant role for mega deals above 250 million. Although there are not so many fewer SaaS deals being closed in Europe – last year there were 4,616, versus 5,040 in the US – the focus remains on smaller rounds. Broadly applicable SaaS niches. Venture capital in Europe mainly flows into a number of recognizable SaaS niches.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: In the US, a large part of the money even goes to rounds above 100 million dollars, with a dominant role for mega deals above 250 million. Although there are not so many fewer SaaS deals being closed in Europe – last year there were 4,616, versus 5,040 in the US – the focus remains on smaller rounds. Broadly applicable SaaS niches. Venture capital in Europe mainly flows into a number of recognizable SaaS niches. The largest category is 'enterprise software' – solutions that make internal processes of organizations more efficient, from HR and planning systems to workflow and ERP tools.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: Although there are not so many fewer SaaS deals being closed in Europe – last year there were 4,616, versus 5,040 in the US – the focus remains on smaller rounds. Broadly applicable SaaS niches. Venture capital in Europe mainly flows into a number of recognizable SaaS niches. The largest category is 'enterprise software' – solutions that make internal processes of organizations more efficient, from HR and planning systems to workflow and ERP tools. For example, the German unicorn Personio managed to further strengthen its position in HR software, and its compatriot N8N specializes in process automation.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: Broadly applicable SaaS niches. Venture capital in Europe mainly flows into a number of recognizable SaaS niches. The largest category is 'enterprise software' – solutions that make internal processes of organizations more efficient, from HR and planning systems to workflow and ERP tools. For example, the German unicorn Personio managed to further strengthen its position in HR software, and its compatriot N8N specializes in process automation. In addition, there are other solutions that can be applied in almost every sector.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: Venture capital in Europe mainly flows into a number of recognizable SaaS niches. The largest category is 'enterprise software' – solutions that make internal processes of organizations more efficient, from HR and planning systems to workflow and ERP tools. For example, the German unicorn Personio managed to further strengthen its position in HR software, and its compatriot N8N specializes in process automation. In addition, there are other solutions that can be applied in almost every sector. Marketing and communication software remains popular, including marketing platform Brevo as an example.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: For example, the French Sekoia.io raised growth capital in 2024 for its AI platform that detects threats in real-time. SaaS for specific industries. SaaS companies also attract a lot of financing in the field of FinTech. For example, the British Volt.io offers a platform that allows companies to also accept payments from international customers, and Qover helps integrate insurance into its service offering across sectors - from mobility to e-commerce. On the other hand, there are parties that operate more specifically at the core of financial service providers, such as Backbase, which offers a customer platform for financial institutions as a replacement for their old IT systems.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: SaaS for specific industries. SaaS companies also attract a lot of financing in the field of FinTech. For example, the British Volt.io offers a platform that allows companies to also accept payments from international customers, and Qover helps integrate insurance into its service offering across sectors - from mobility to e-commerce. On the other hand, there are parties that operate more specifically at the core of financial service providers, such as Backbase, which offers a customer platform for financial institutions as a replacement for their old IT systems. It is an example of one of the many 'vertical' SaaS services, designed for a specific sector.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: SaaS companies also attract a lot of financing in the field of FinTech. For example, the British Volt.io offers a platform that allows companies to also accept payments from international customers, and Qover helps integrate insurance into its service offering across sectors - from mobility to e-commerce. On the other hand, there are parties that operate more specifically at the core of financial service providers, such as Backbase, which offers a customer platform for financial institutions as a replacement for their old IT systems. It is an example of one of the many 'vertical' SaaS services, designed for a specific sector. In healthcare, Doctolib enters the broader European market with a platform that facilitates appointment management, consultations and patient communication, and Unmind raised growth funding for a platform that helps company employees improve their mental health.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: For example, the British Volt.io offers a platform that allows companies to also accept payments from international customers, and Qover helps integrate insurance into its service offering across sectors - from mobility to e-commerce. On the other hand, there are parties that operate more specifically at the core of financial service providers, such as Backbase, which offers a customer platform for financial institutions as a replacement for their old IT systems. It is an example of one of the many 'vertical' SaaS services, designed for a specific sector. In healthcare, Doctolib enters the broader European market with a platform that facilitates appointment management, consultations and patient communication, and Unmind raised growth funding for a platform that helps company employees improve their mental health. In hospitality, the Dutch Mews raised new capital to roll out its platform internationally, and in construction and real estate, PlanRadar previously did this with its project management software.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: In the US, development is going even faster; There, AI-SaaS consumes almost 80 percent of all SaaS funding. Nevertheless, Europe is producing a lot of innovative, 'AI-first' SaaS companies that specialize in all kinds of niches. The Netherlands even has an above-average number of AI-SaaS deals and funding. For example, Cradle developed an AI model that can design new proteins and sell them to laboratories as a SaaS service. Compatriot Fourthline focuses on identity fraud and compliance in the financial sector with AI, and FRISS does the same for insurance risks.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: Nevertheless, Europe is producing a lot of innovative, 'AI-first' SaaS companies that specialize in all kinds of niches. The Netherlands even has an above-average number of AI-SaaS deals and funding. For example, Cradle developed an AI model that can design new proteins and sell them to laboratories as a SaaS service. Compatriot Fourthline focuses on identity fraud and compliance in the financial sector with AI, and FRISS does the same for insurance risks. TestGorilla makes selection processes smarter and less sensitive to bias, and Sympower uses AI to keep electricity grids in balance.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: The Netherlands even has an above-average number of AI-SaaS deals and funding. For example, Cradle developed an AI model that can design new proteins and sell them to laboratories as a SaaS service. Compatriot Fourthline focuses on identity fraud and compliance in the financial sector with AI, and FRISS does the same for insurance risks. TestGorilla makes selection processes smarter and less sensitive to bias, and Sympower uses AI to keep electricity grids in balance. The German Accure offers a platform for AI analysis of battery data in, among others, the mobility and industrial sector, in order to improve the safety and lifespan of batteries and accumulators.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: For example, Cradle developed an AI model that can design new proteins and sell them to laboratories as a SaaS service. Compatriot Fourthline focuses on identity fraud and compliance in the financial sector with AI, and FRISS does the same for insurance risks. TestGorilla makes selection processes smarter and less sensitive to bias, and Sympower uses AI to keep electricity grids in balance. The German Accure offers a platform for AI analysis of battery data in, among others, the mobility and industrial sector, in order to improve the safety and lifespan of batteries and accumulators. TestGorilla makes selection processes smarter and less sensitive to bias, and Sympower uses AI to keep electricity grids in balance. metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: TestGorilla makes selection processes smarter and less sensitive to bias, and Sympower uses AI to keep electricity grids in balance. The German Accure offers a platform for AI analysis of battery data in, among others, the mobility and industrial sector, in order to improve the safety and lifespan of batteries and accumulators. The British Synthesia automates the production of corporate films and training videos with generative AI and raised $180 million at the beginning of 2025 at a valuation of more than $2 billion. And from Sweden, Lovable is experiencing enormous growth with a platform that uses AI to accelerate software development and make it more accessible. The company was founded in 2023 and reached a valuation of almost $2 billion in just a few years.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

And from Sweden, Lovable is experiencing enormous growth with a platform that uses AI to accelerate software development and make it more accessible. The company was founded in 2023 and reached a valuation of almost $2 billion in just a few years. Major AI developers are setting the tone.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: Doctolib supports doctors and patients with appointments and communication; an essential, but often suboptimal digitalized process. In addition, opportunities lie in niches that large but generic AI models cannot handle. For example, Cradle, which focuses entirely on generative AI for protein design, is trained on a huge database of protein structures and properties, and combines deep knowledge of biology with technical capabilities. metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: In addition, opportunities lie in niches that large but generic AI models cannot handle. For example, Cradle, which focuses entirely on generative AI for protein design, is trained on a huge database of protein structures and properties, and combines deep knowledge of biology with technical capabilities. Sympower developed an AI solution that balances electricity grids and connects both large consumers and energy producers. Collaborating with parties in energy-intensive industries such as agriculture, cement production and data centers requires strong relationships and a lot of domain knowledge that cannot be quickly copied. Finally, it is striking that successful SaaS companies often form a connecting layer between multiple parties.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: For example, Cradle, which focuses entirely on generative AI for protein design, is trained on a huge database of protein structures and properties, and combines deep knowledge of biology with technical capabilities. Sympower developed an AI solution that balances electricity grids and connects both large consumers and energy producers. Collaborating with parties in energy-intensive industries such as agriculture, cement production and data centers requires strong relationships and a lot of domain knowledge that cannot be quickly copied. Finally, it is striking that successful SaaS companies often form a connecting layer between multiple parties. For example, N8N's automation platform allows different software packages to 'talk' to each other - from CRM systems and email tools to databases and cloud services.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: Sympower developed an AI solution that balances electricity grids and connects both large consumers and energy producers. Collaborating with parties in energy-intensive industries such as agriculture, cement production and data centers requires strong relationships and a lot of domain knowledge that cannot be quickly copied. Finally, it is striking that successful SaaS companies often form a connecting layer between multiple parties. For example, N8N's automation platform allows different software packages to 'talk' to each other - from CRM systems and email tools to databases and cloud services. In this way, users can automate complex work processes, across different applications.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: Collaborating with parties in energy-intensive industries such as agriculture, cement production and data centers requires strong relationships and a lot of domain knowledge that cannot be quickly copied. Finally, it is striking that successful SaaS companies often form a connecting layer between multiple parties. For example, N8N's automation platform allows different software packages to 'talk' to each other - from CRM systems and email tools to databases and cloud services. In this way, users can automate complex work processes, across different applications. Qover offers a connecting layer in the insurance world; Through APIs, the company makes it possible to directly integrate insurance products into, for example, mobility apps or e-commerce platforms.

The European SaaS market is full of innovation, but the path to global dominance is anything but self-evident. The continent faces a structural scalability gap and a more cautious investment culture. metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: The way forward. The European SaaS market is full of innovation, but the path to global dominance is anything but self-evident. The continent faces a structural scalability gap and a more cautious investment culture. However, encouraged by a geopolitical need to also become more technologically self-sufficient, it is taking several steps in the right direction. On September 23, 2025, Minister Karremans presented a list of [measures](https://www.rijksoverheid.nl/actueel/nieuws/2025/09/23/meer-groei-nederlandse-startups-en-scale-ups-door-meer-capital-talent-en-projecten-techleap) to support “the tech champions of tomorrow”, with an emphasis on research-intensive key technologies such as cybersecurity, quantum computing and artificial intelligence.

metadata.source_title: Europe's software market looking for scale and niches

text: The European SaaS market is full of innovation, but the path to global dominance is anything but self-evident. The continent faces a structural scalability gap and a more cautious investment culture. However, encouraged by a geopolitical need to also become more technologically self-sufficient, it is taking several steps in the right direction. On September 23, 2025, Minister Karremans presented a list of [measures](https://www.rijksoverheid.nl/actueel/nieuws/2025/09/23/meer-groei-nederlandse-startups-en-scale-ups-door-meer-capital-talent-en-projecten-techleap) to support “the tech champions of tomorrow”, with an emphasis on research-intensive key technologies such as cybersecurity, quantum computing and artificial intelligence. For example, the outgoing cabinet is investing 200 million euros in a second fund within the European Tech Champions Initiative (ETCI), and Invest-NL is exploring ways to bring more pension capital to venture capital funds.

For example, the outgoing cabinet is investing 200 million euros in a second fund within the European Tech Champions Initiative (ETCI), and Invest-NL is exploring ways to bring more pension capital to venture capital funds. In any case, the current generation of SaaS companies shows that there are plenty of opportunities.

A focus on business-critical processes, AI applications in specific niches, and building a connecting layer are strategies with which local players can position themselves strongly, even amid the growth violence of American parties.
